title: Puerto Banus Sunset Catamaran Hire: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Puerto Banus offers one of the Mediterranean's finest sunset experiences, and private catamaran hire from the marina's central pontoons provides an unforgettable evening. Prices typically range from €1,500 for a half-day charter, depending on vessel size and catering. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local owners for verified bookings.

How much does it cost to rent a private catamaran in Puerto Banus?

Sunset catamaran hire in Puerto Banus starts from around €1,500 for a half-day and reaches €4,000 for a full-day luxury charter. Prices depend on boat size, included extras such as catering and water toys, and the season. For specific rates you can hire a boat directly from owner-listed options on BoatHire24, ensuring transparency and no hidden fees.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

For bareboat catamaran hire in Spain, a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent is typically required, but many sunset charters come with a professional skipper. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking, so you can enjoy the sunset without holding a licence on crewed vessels. BoatHire24 listings clearly indicate whether a skipper is included.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. The platform simplifies booking and payment, handling the logistics so you can focus on relaxing. In 2026, demand for sunset charters in Puerto Banus continues to grow, making early booking advisable.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

Most catamaran sunset charters depart from the main Puerto Banus marina, a purpose-built harbour lined with upmarket boutiques and restaurants. Nearby alternative departure points include Marbella's Real Club Náutico, La Carihuela in Benalmádena, and the marina at Estepona. Further afield, the exclusive enclave of Sotogrande offers another option for those seeking a quieter setting. A typical sunset cruise heads west along the Costa del Sol towards Cabo de Trafalgar or around the Bay of Marbella. Some itineraries include a stop near the island of Gibraltar or a slow cruise past the hills of Mijas. The sea here is the Mediterranean Sea, and its calm late-afternoon conditions are ideal for catamaran sailing.

Which boat to rent

  1. Luxury motor yacht, Best for a glamorous sunset party with up to 12 guests, from €2,500 half-day.
  2. Sailing catamaran, Best for a peaceful family sunset sail with up to 10 guests, from €1,800 half-day. Consider the Shangani for hire, a 70ft model known for its stability and deck space.
  3. RIB or sport boat, Best for an active sunset excursion with water sports for up to 8 guests, from €900 half-day.
  4. Gulets and traditional wooden boats, Best for a stylish group dinner at anchor for up to 20 guests, from €3,000 half-day.
  5. Azimut or Sunseeker flybridge, Best for a sophisticated couple's evening with top-tier service for up to 6 guests, from €3,500 half-day.

Costs: what to expect

  • Shared sunset tour: from €90 per person, typical 3-hour cruise with welcome drink.
  • Small motorboat half-day: from €500 for two hours, self-drive with outboard.
  • Catamaran full-day with skipper: from €2,500 for up to 10 guests, including fuel.
  • Luxury crewed yacht full-day: from €5,000 for up to 12 guests, with gourmet catering.

For a clear breakdown of all fees and to compare options, refer to how boat rental works on the BoatHire24 site. The platform's integrated payment system holds deposits securely until after your trip.

Season and local tips

The high season for sunset charters in Puerto Banus runs from June to September, when the evening Levante wind typically drops after 18:00, creating glassy conditions. Spring and autumn offer quieter marinas and slightly lower prices.

For the best colours, book a sunset charter that departs at least 90 minutes before local sunset time; the light over the Mediterranean can paint the sky for an hour. Many skippers recommend anchoring off Marbella's Cabopino dunes for a spectacular horizon view.

At a glance

  • Price band: €900, €5,000 half-day
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 2, 4 hours sunset cruise
  • Capacity: 6, 12 guests typical
  • Currency: EUR
  • Skipper: included on most crewed charters

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I book a last-minute sunset catamaran in Puerto Banus?

Last-minute availability exists but is limited in high season, so booking at least a week ahead is recommended for the best choice.

What is included in a typical catamaran sunset charter?

Most charters include a professional skipper, fuel, basic refreshments, and use of onboard facilities such as shaded seating and a sound system.

Are there sunset catamarans that depart from other towns near Puerto Banus?

Yes, skippers based in Marbella, Estepona, and Benalmádena also offer sunset trips and can often pick up from Puerto Banus for an additional fee.

If you own a catamaran or yacht in Puerto Banus, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to rent out your boat to thousands of sunset-seeking visitors. The platform takes care of marketing, payments, and customer support, leaving you to enjoy the summer evenings.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
